title: The Forgotten Grave.
content:
After a hundred years
    Nobody knows the place, --
    Agony, that enacted there,
    Motionless as peace.

    Weeds triumphant ranged,
    Strangers strolled and spelled
    At the lone orthography
    Of the elder dead.

    Winds of summer fields
    Recollect the way, --
    Instinct picking up the key
    Dropped by memory.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "The Forgotten Grave.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
